evals.prompt: I need to create a new Adobe App Builder single-page application with React. It should be an Experience Cloud extension that integrates with AEM Content Fragments.
evals.expected_output: Project initialized using the AEM CF Console template with React SPA, proper ext.config.yaml configured, and npm dependencies installed.

assertions.description: Creates the AEM Content Fragment Console extension manifest.
Registers the AEM Content Fragment Console extension in the root app manifest.
Defines extension operations in ext.config.yaml.
Includes the React SPA frontend directory for the extension.
Includes a React entry point for the frontend.
Builds the generated App Builder project successfully.

evals.prompt: Set up a headless App Builder project — just Runtime actions, no UI. I need an action that calls an external REST API.
evals.expected_output: Project initialized using the excshell or bare template, web-assets and web-src manifest references removed or not added, and a single action scaffolded with HTTP fetch capability.

assertions.description: Does not create a root-level web frontend directory for a headless app.
Does not keep template-generated web-src directories in a headless result.
Creates a Runtime actions directory.
Scaffolds at least one action implementation file.
Defines at least one Runtime action under application.runtimeManifest.
Removes stale web-src/frontend references from app.config.yaml for headless projects.
Builds the generated headless App Builder project successfully.

evals.prompt: Create an API Mesh configuration for my project that proxies requests to two backend services.
evals.expected_output: Project initialized using the API Mesh template, mesh configuration file created with source handlers for two services.

assertions.description: Creates an API Mesh configuration file.
Defines at least two source handlers in the mesh configuration.
Builds the API Mesh project successfully.

evals.prompt: I want to build an Asset Compute worker that generates custom renditions for AEM Assets.
evals.expected_output: Project initialized using the Asset Compute template, worker file created, test framework available.

assertions.description: Creates an Asset Compute worker implementation file.
Includes the Asset Compute worker test directory.
Builds the Asset Compute project successfully.
Runs the generated Asset Compute worker tests successfully.

evals.prompt: Just set up a bare App Builder project — I'll configure everything myself from scratch.
evals.expected_output: Project initialized using the bare template with minimal structure, no pre-configured actions or web assets.

assertions.description: Creates the root App Builder manifest.
Keeps the source tree minimal for a bare project scaffold.
Builds the bare App Builder project successfully.
